findings.claim: On branch followup26, `devtools::check(--as-cran)` reports FAIL 15 that are PRE-EXISTING (proven by A/B stash: identical FAIL 15 + identical failing files on the clean baseline) — registry-split (.ptr_registry env NULL in options() under the check sandbox) + pp-toggles (vignette readLines / comparator / e2e absent from the sandbox); `devtools::test()` 0/0/0 serial remains the authoritative gate, NOT check.
findings.detail: Reaffirms memory `rcmd-check-preexisting-failures` on this branch with the A/B method: `git stash push -u`, run check on baseline, compare FAIL count + files, `git stash pop`. PASS differs (baseline 2696 vs tree 2725) only because the change adds new passing tests; FAIL count identical = zero new failures.
findings.source: devtools::check(--as-cran) on followup26 @ b6a2f72 parent; test-rewrite-registry.R:259-264, test-pp-toggles-vignette.R, test-pp-toggles-comparator.R, test-pp-toggles-e2e.R
findings.verification: A/B run this session: tree check FAIL 15 == baseline (git stash push -u) check FAIL 15, identical failing files (test-rewrite-registry .ptr_registry-anchor :257; test-pp-toggles-{vignette,comparator,e2e}). PASS differed (2725 vs 2696) only by the new passing tests => 0 new failures.

findings.claim: A behavior-preserving refactor needs BOTH a RED contract-test layer for the new surface (red-first proof) AND a GREEN characterization layer for the preserved top-level behavior; red-first cannot apply to preserved behavior, so the green layer's teeth must be proven by MUTATION (deliberately break each derivation, confirm the matching green test flips red).
findings.detail: In /plan-to-test the green regression tests must observe the behavior the refactor preserves, not the untouched machinery — else they are vacuous (a test of an untouched algorithm stays green regardless). Also: 4 negative tests came up falsely green at red-first because bare `expect_error()` caught "could not find function" — pin negative tests to a contract-derived message substring so absence-of-impl can't satisfy them.
findings.source: this session: /plan-to-test red layer (test-partition-view.R) + green layer (test-partition-view-regression.R); .claude/skills/plan-to-test
findings.verification: Both layers exist: test-partition-view.R (18 red contract test_thats), test-partition-view-regression.R (3 green). Mutation teeth-proof executed this session (FAIL 4/7/9 per broken derivation); 4 false-green negative tests fixed by pinning message matchers.

findings.claim: `git checkout -- <file>` cannot restore an UNTRACKED new file — it errors "pathspec did not match", so a mutation-test loop that mutates a brand-new (uncommitted) source file and relies on git checkout to revert will silently STACK mutations instead of reverting.
findings.detail: Detected because the post-loop "restored" re-run still showed failures; recovery was rewriting the function bodies by hand. For mutation-testing a new file, back it up with `cp` first (or `git add` it once) and restore from that, not git checkout.
findings.source: this session: mutation-testing R/paintr-partition-view.R (untracked); git checkout -- <file> failed 'did not match any file(s)'
findings.verification: Observed this session: git checkout -- R/paintr-partition-view.R (untracked) errored 'pathspec did not match any file(s)'; the perl mutations stacked instead of reverting. Consistent with git semantics: checkout restores only index-tracked paths.

findings.claim: The shared-consumer binder's `host_owned_keys` argument is the COMPLEMENT set ("keys this surface must NOT bind because some other surface owns them"), not the set this surface owns — the binder binds `setdiff(names(resolutions), host_owned_keys)`.
findings.detail: Each surface computes the complement differently: single-instance `ptr_app` → character(0) (binds every key); panel host (L2) → setdiff(consumer_keys, panel_keys) (net: binds the >=2-formula panel keys); embed instance → names(dots$shared) i.e. the coordinator's panel keys (net: binds its formula-local keys). The name reads backwards from its meaning, which is exactly why the three sites were easy to compute inconsistently.
findings.source: R/paintr-server.R:3376 (ptr_bind_local_shared_consumers: keys <- setdiff(names(resolutions), host_owned_keys)); R/paintr-app.R:339, R/paintr-app.R:1164; R/paintr-shared-ui.R:416
findings.verification: Re-verified at HEAD a1737f7: the binder still does keys <- setdiff(names(resolutions), host_owned_keys) at R/paintr-server.R:3390 (cited 3376 — drifted, claim holds). As of b6a2f72 (ADR 0026 #11) the three per-surface complement VALUES are now produced centrally by partition_host_owned_keys() (R/paintr-partition-view.R): single->character(0), panel_host->setdiff(consumer_keys,panel_keys), instance->panel_keys — the exact three values this finding lists, so the claim holds verbatim. The detail's 'the three sites were easy to compute inconsistently' is now historical: they query the shared helper (R/paintr-app.R:339,1166; R/paintr-shared-ui.R:416).

findings.claim: `names()` on an empty/unnamed list returns NULL, so the embed-instance site passes `panel_keys = NULL` into new_partition_view; an `assert_that(is.character(panel_keys))` rejects NULL, so the constructor must normalize NULL→character(0) BEFORE asserting (keeping the type-check teeth for genuinely-wrong types like integer).
findings.detail: Caught by M-SHR.1 (bare ptr_server, no shared) which drives the empty-shared embed path — a leaf characterization test that pins exactly this NULL boundary. The original inline code tolerated NULL because setdiff(x, NULL) == setdiff(x, character(0)).
findings.source: R/paintr-partition-view.R (new_partition_view: panel_keys <- panel_keys %||% character()); R/paintr-app.R:1164 (names(dots$shared %||% list())); tests/testthat/test-module-server-shared.R (M-SHR.1)
findings.verification: partition-view.R:43-44 normalizes panel_keys/consumer_keys via %||% character() before assert_that(is.character); M-SHR.1 (test-module-server-shared.R:14) drives the empty-shared embed path that exposed the NULL.

findings.claim: `partition_view` (internal, pure, non-reactive) is now the single origin for shared-key ownership: `new_partition_view(role, panel_keys, consumer_keys)` with role in {single, panel_host, instance}, and `partition_host_owned_keys(view)` / `partition_renders_section(view)` replace the three hand-derived host_owned_keys complements + the render_shared_section boolean.
findings.detail: role→complement mapping: single=character(0), panel_host=setdiff(consumer_keys,panel_keys), instance=panel_keys. The partition ALGORITHM (shared_partition / panel_keys = keys in >=2 formulas) is untouched — this seam is the CONSUMPTION side only. Behavior is byte-stable (each accessor returns exactly the literal each call site used to compute inline).
findings.verification: R/paintr-partition-view.R defines new_partition_view/partition_host_owned_keys/partition_renders_section; switch mapping single=character(0)/panel_host=setdiff/instance=view$panel_keys at :70-72; all 4 call sites rewired.

findings.claim: `render_shared_section = TRUE` is passed at exactly one site (app.R:169, single-instance ptr_app); every other call passes FALSE — so it is a pure function of surface role and folds to `partition_renders_section(view) == identical(role, "single")`.
findings.verification: app.R:169 is the only render_shared_section TRUE site (now partition_renders_section(new_partition_view('single'))); folds to identical(role,'single') at partition-view.R:83.

findings.claim: `test-shared-server-partition.R` is a faithful GREEN top-level guard for the panel-host and embed-instance ownership surfaces with real teeth for a consumption-side refactor: S-P2.4(b) asserts panel_html owns `shared_B` but `expect_no_match` `shared_A`/`shared_C`; S-P2.2/S-P2.4(c) assert grid_html has `plot_1-shared_A` but not `plot_2-shared_A`.
findings.detail: Teeth come from observing the host_owned_keys-driven BINDING/RENDERING split — NOT `obj$panel_keys` (the untouched algorithm), which would stay green no matter how the consumption refactor breaks. Mutating panel_host→character(0) flipped 7 tests red; instance→character(0) flipped 9 red — confirming the discrimination.
findings.source: tests/testthat/test-shared-server-partition.R (S-P2.2, S-P2.4(b)(c)); uses ptr_app_grid_components + shiny::testServer, not bare ptr_server_internal
findings.verification: test-shared-server-partition.R S-P2.2 (:89), S-P2.4 (:143) use ptr_app_grid_components + grid_html expect_match plot_1-shared_A / expect_no_match plot_2-shared_A (:176-179). Mutation this session: panel_host->character(0) flipped FAIL 7, instance->character(0) flipped FAIL 9.

findings.claim: The single-instance `ptr_app` producer path (render_shared_section=TRUE @ app.R:169, host_owned_keys=character(0) @ app.R:339) had NO non-browser green guard before this work; `ptr_app_components(f)$ui` is the faithful harness — assert the inline `shared_<key>` id is present and the coordinator panel's `ptr_shared_draw_all` id is absent.
findings.detail: `ptr_shared_draw_all` is emitted ONLY when a standalone shared panel is built (verified: ptr_ui_shared_panel(ptr_shared(list(f1,f2))) contains it), so `expect_no_match` of it discriminates a wrongful single-instance→panel promotion. Mutating render_shared_section→FALSE flipped REG-1/REG-2 red.
findings.verification: test-partition-view-regression.R REG-1/2/3 use ptr_app_components(f)$ui/$server; REG-2 expect_no_match ptr_shared_draw_all (:65). Probe confirmed the coordinator panel DOES emit ptr_shared_draw_all (discriminating). Mutation: renders_section->FALSE flipped FAIL 4.

findings.claim: Panel-owned SOURCE ownership is not a separately hand-wired channel — sources derive ownership from the same `panel_keys` partition and are delivered via `panel_sources` (a reactive list from `ptr_setup_panel_sources(obj)`) threaded through the binder, not via a `host_owned_keys`-style setdiff.
findings.detail: This bounds the partition_view scope: its ownership PREDICATE covers consumer/value keys (the host_owned_keys + render_shared_section question); source delivery stays on panel_sources unchanged. So "the partition is role-agnostic" (CONTEXT.md/ADR 0023) and "the view's predicate is consumer-key-only" are not in conflict — panel_keys itself is the role-agnostic origin both consult.
findings.source: R/paintr-shared-ui.R:400 (ptr_setup_panel_sources), :416 (panel_sources threaded into binder); CONTEXT.md (partition role-agnostic, ADR 0023)
findings.verification: shared-ui.R:400 builds panel_sources via ptr_setup_panel_sources(obj) and threads it into the binder; no host_owned_keys-style setdiff exists for sources.
